stories_covered: Anthropic $30B raise at $380B post-money (up from $10B ask)
Anthropic now at 64% of OpenAI enterprise revenue despite being smaller
OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI
Stripe ($140B valuation private) vs Adyen ($50B public, 21% growth)
AppLoving down 70%, Shopify getting no love
harry_takes: Narrative momentum completely shifted from SaaS to AI; gravity well pulling everything else down
Enterprises will will AI into existence over next 1-2 years regardless of ROI because CEOs see it as mandatory agenda item
Public software stocks trading at 8-10x growth but may be oversold; Shopify/Navan not deserving of dismay if AI adoption is real
Canary in coal mine: if enterprises say 'great AI, now slowing spend' in 1-2 years, that's the bear case to watch
harry_boldest_quote: Corporate America has decided they're going to make this bet.
predictions: Jason: OpenAI revenue went 5% to 64% of Anthropic in 14 months—that's budget theft even if total budget growing
Harry: Companies paying 50x run-rate revenues must grow 300-400% for 3-4 years to justify valuation (Harvey example)
Jason: By end of year Fortune 500 will be knee-deep in AI replacement decisions, growth numbers will be jaw-dropping
Rory: Back-end 2026 we'll see Anthropic, OpenAI, SpaceX dive for public markets due to capex needs
disagreements: Yes—Rory urges valuation caution (Anthropic is 50x run-rate, needs to triple then double); Jason and Harry argue momentum is irreversible for 12-24 months. Rory skeptical of 'presumption of success' for both AI models.
harry_role: Driving the 'gravity well' narrative, bullish on AI momentum short-term, somewhat dismissive of public SaaS bears, synthesizing capital flows into AI-only investing thesis.
notable: Harry's gravity metaphor: everything getting pulled down except the escape-velocity companies (Anthropic, OpenAI); capital concentration on two-three winners unprecedented in venture, leaving all others unsellable.
